word_list.pronunciation: kňą…™k wňą Ćnz fňąiňźt ňą Ćp

senses.definition: to lie on a sofa, bed, etc. in order to become less tense or anxious

examples.x: After such a long week, all I wanted to do for the evening was kick my feet up and watch some movies with a big bowl of popcorn.
He's kicking his feet up onto the blankets, not even bothering to take off his shoes.
After a long day at work, I can't wait to go home, kick my feet up, and watch my favorite TV show.
During the weekend, he likes to kick his feet up, read a good book, and forget about his work responsibilities.
The vacation cabin by the lake is the perfect place to kick our feet up, unwind, and enjoy the peaceful surroundings.

senses.definition: to take moment to relax and appreciate what life has to offer, particularly when one is feeling overwhelmed

examples.x: You've been working so hard lately; it's time to take a break and smell the roses.
In our fast-paced world, people often forget to stop and smell the roses.
Instead of constantly worrying about the future, why not take a moment to smell the roses today?
She realized she needed to slow down and start smelling the roses.
Let's take a break and go smell the roses in the garden; it will help clear our minds.

senses.definition: the renewal of one's energy or endurance that allows one to continue or start over a physical exertion

examples.x: She gained a second wind during the campaign and turned the opinion polls around.
After feeling exhausted in the first half of the game, the team found their second wind and went on to win.
During the marathon, she hit a wall at the 20-mile mark but soon caught her second wind and finished strong.
I was ready to give up on the project, but then I got a second wind and completed it ahead of schedule.
He felt like he couldn't keep going, but the encouragement from the crowd gave him a second wind.

senses.definition: to put an stop to any activity one was doing in order to take a short break and relax a bit

examples.x: After sprinting to catch the train, I needed to sit down and catch my breath.
The thrilling rollercoaster ride left me breathless, and I had to stop to catch my breath.
During the heated argument, she paused to catch her breath and collect her thoughts.
Following the intense dance performance, the dancers gathered backstage to catch their breath.
The unexpected news was so shocking that it took him a while to catch his breath and respond.

senses.definition: to give one's best effort in work or activities, followed by taking time to rest
to relax and take a rest in order to recover one's lost energy

examples.x: Right now, I am recharging my batteries with a cup of tea and a good book.
Every weekend, she recharges her batteries by spending time with her family.
Last summer, I recharged my batteries with a relaxing vacation by the seaside.
After a demanding workweek, I like to spend the weekend at the beach to recharge my batteries.
A good night's sleep can do wonders to recharge your batteries and help you face the day ahead.

senses.definition: to go for a walk, particularly after spending a long time sitting in one spot

examples.x: During the long flight, it's essential to stretch your legs to prevent discomfort and maintain circulation.
After hours of studying, I like to take a break and stretch my legs by walking around the neighborhood.
In the middle of the road trip, we pulled over to a rest area to stretch our legs and grab a snack.
Working at a desk all day can be tiring, so I make sure to stretch my legs and take short walks to stay refreshed.
After the meeting, she invited her colleagues to stretch their legs by going for a short hike in the nearby park.

senses.definition: to behave in a way one desires without worrying about other's opinions

examples.x: She lets herself go and dances freely at the party, expressing her joy without concern for how others perceive her.
They let themselves go during the weekend getaway, enjoying leisurely activities, disconnecting from work, and prioritizing their relaxation.
After years of strict dieting, she decided to let herself go and enjoy her favorite indulgent desserts.
Following a tough workweek, he lets himself go during the weekend, indulging in his hobbies and favorite pastimes.

word_list.pronunciation: lňą…õt wňą Ćnz hňą…õ…Ļ dňąa än

senses.definition: to behave in a comfortable manner, without worrying about one's problems or other people's opinions

examples.x: After the intense week at work, she couldn't wait to let her hair down and enjoy a relaxing weekend.
At the party, she finally let her hair down, dancing and having a great time with her friends.
On vacation, he decided to let his hair down and try new activities he'd never experienced before.
After a week of intense work, she's looking forward to letting her hair down and having a night of fun with friends.
The retirement party was a memorable evening, with the retiree finally letting their hair down and celebrating decades of hard work.

senses.definition: to refresh oneself, usually by going outside or doing some physical activities

examples.x: The changes cleared away the cobwebs and stirred the survivors to keener effort.
We have a cottage in the Cotswolds, and getting back there after a few days in London really blows away the cobwebs.
A brisk walk in the morning helps me blow away the cobwebs and prepare for the day.
After a short break, I can clear away the cobwebs and approach the problem with a fresh perspective.

senses.definition: fully drained of strength and energy

examples.x: After working a double shift at the hospital, she was ready to drop from exhaustion.
During the marathon, many runners were ready to drop by the time they reached the finish line.
After a long day of hiking, I was fit to drop, and I couldn't wait to get some rest.
The students were ready to drop after studying for their final exams all night.

senses.definition: to feel relief or relaxation because a period of worry, stress, or anticipation has passed

examples.x: After days of uncertainty, they finally received good news and could breathe a sigh of relief.
When the test results came back negative, she breathed a sigh of relief.
After weeks of uncertainty, the deal was finally signed, and both parties breathed a sigh of relief.
